retrieved_docs.title: Developmental Disturbances of the Teeth, Anomalies of Structure -- History and Physical
retrieved_docs.content: Dens evaginatus, or a “talon cusp,” presents as an outcropped growth of normal tooth structure, commonly affecting the lingual aspect of an anterior maxillary tooth. Talon cusps have been classified into three categories with distinct clinical presentations. [17] Type 1 talon cusps are a morphologically well-differentiated additional cusp that extends half the distance from the cementoenamel junction to the incisal edge. Type 2 talon cusps, known as a “semi-talon,” measure at least one millimeter and extend half the distance from the cementoenamel junction to the incisal edge. Type 2 talon cusps often blend with the surface of the crown. Type 3 talon cusps, or “trace talons,” are enlarged or prominent cingula that can be conical, bifid, or tubercle.

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Technique -- Traditional technique complications
retrieved_docs.content: Craniocervical perforation may develop when the convenience extension is left unfinished before the bur shaft enters the canal space. [63] [61] [62] Pulp debris left behind during preparation may also discolor the crown, especially when the access cavity lacks proper incisal extension and lies too close to the gingival margin. [63] [62] [40] Misjudgment of the tooth's lingual-axial or mesial-axial inclination can result in abrasion of the labial or distal wall during instrumentation. [63] [61] [62] Failure to complete the convenience extension can create a ledge at the apical-labial curve, where the instrument shaft rests on the shoulder or cavity edge instead of advancing smoothly into the canal. [63] [61] [64]

retrieved_docs.title: Developmental Disturbances of the Teeth, Anomalies of Structure -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: Dens evaginatus, also known as a talon cusp, results from a developmental tubercle, often seen on the lingual aspect of the tooth structure; however, it can occur on the occlusal surface. A coronal anomaly, these accessory cusps typically project from the cingulum or cementoenamel junction of anterior teeth. [1] The etiology of this condition is attributed to genetic and/or environmental factors, such as trauma to the developing tooth bud or local factors influencing the morphodifferentiation during tooth development. [2]

retrieved_docs.title: Anatomy, Occlusal Contact Relations And Mandibular Movements -- Embryology
retrieved_docs.content: Odontogenesis is the formation of teeth. The deciduous or baby teeth develop at 6 to 8 weeks gestation, whereas succedaneous teeth develop at 20 weeks. The ectoderm forms the dental enamel, while the ectomesenchyme (neural crest-derived) forms the dentin, cementum, periodontal ligament, and pulp. Placodes represent invaginations of primary dental lamina within the mandibular and maxillary arches beginning the process of tooth formation. Tooth buds form from the placodes, and the tooth buds develop into teeth. [11]

retrieved_docs.title: Developmental Disturbances of the Teeth, Anomalies of Structure -- Epidemiology
retrieved_docs.content: Dens invaginatus has no gender predilection and a prevalence between 0.3 to 10%, depending on the population studied. [9] This condition is mainly identified on the maxillary lateral incisors, followed by the maxillary central incisors. It is very rarely discovered in any other teeth. [10]

retrieved_docs.title: Excessive Gingival Display -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: Anterior dentoalveolar extrusion is the overeruption of the maxillary incisors, resulting in excessive gingival display due to a more coronal position of the gingival margins. [1] Tooth wear and an anterior deep bite may be the causes of the anterior extrusion. A compensatory incisor overeruption is seen in cases of anterior tooth wear. [1] A discrepancy between the anterior and posterior occlusal planes is usually found in deep bite cases. [1]

retrieved_docs.title: Developmental Disturbances of the Teeth, Anomalies of Shape and Size -- Complications
retrieved_docs.content: Macrodontia often presents an aesthetic concern in the anterior maxilla, while macrodonts in the posterior region may contribute to crowding and impede normal tooth eruption. Radiographic monitoring is essential to assess potential eruption abnormalities.

retrieved_docs.title: Embryology, Teeth -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: Taurodontism: It is also referred to as bull's teeth and is *characterized by teeth with abnormally large pulp chambers with furcation displaced apically, resulting in larger than normal apical-occlusal height. Molars are the most commonly affected teeth. It frequently correlates with syndromes such as Klinefelter's syndrome, Mohr syndrome, orofacial digital syndrome, and Down syndrome. Peg laterals: It is commonly seen in the maxillary anterior region affecting the maxillary lateral incisor and is characterized by a tooth that is conical in shape with a taper in the incisal region.

retrieved_docs.title: Stainless Steel Crowns in Primary Dentition -- Technique or Treatment -- Proximal Reduction
retrieved_docs.content: Proximal surfaces are reduced using a 69 L/ tapered fissure bur at high speed to make room for the crown and establish a feather finish line gingivally while avoiding damaging neighboring teeth. The mesial and distal contact points should be cleared so a probe could pass through them. Following the normal proximal contour, proximal slices should slightly converge toward the occlusal and lingual. A smooth taper from occlusal to gingival, free of ledges or shoulders, should be achieved. [10] [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Embryology, Teeth -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Fusion may lead to crowding, occlusal disturbances, and esthetic problems. Since the fused teeth have a greater surface area and root mass, resorption may become further delayed, leading to delayed or ectopic eruption of its permanent successor.

retrieved_docs.title: Excessive Gingival Display -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: Teeth erupt in an active phase, where the tooth reaches its occlusal position, and a passive phase, where the gingival tissues migrate apically, exposing the crown. [5] Passive eruption develops in 4 stages: Junctional epithelium on the enamel Junctional epithelium in part on enamel and cementum apical to the cementoenamel junction (CEJ) Junctional epithelium is entirely on the cementum and the sulcus' base at the CEJ Stage 3 and a part of the root is clinically exposed. [3]

retrieved_docs.title: Developmental Disturbances of the Teeth, Anomalies of Shape and Size -- Epidemiology
retrieved_docs.content: Microdontia occurs more frequently in female individuals, with an overall incidence of 1.5% to 2%. [11] The maxillary lateral incisor is most commonly affected, often referred to as a "peg lateral." [12] Macrodontia has no sex predilection and has been reported in permanent teeth, with a prevalence ranging from 0.03% to 1.9%. [13] This condition typically presents in individuals aged 8 to 13, coinciding with the eruption of permanent dentition.

retrieved_docs.title: Embryology, Teeth -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: Dens invaginatus: It is also referred to as dens in dente or extensive compound odontoma or pregnant women anomaly and is characterized by invagination on the external surface of the tooth crown. It occurs before the calcification begins. The degree of invagination may vary from a short pit in the crown region to a deep invagination extending into the root portion or, in rare cases, even beyond the apex. A maxillary lateral incisor is the most commonly affected tooth. It can be easily identified on the radiograph by the pear-shaped invagination and narrow constriction at the opening. [13]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 1 -- Community 250
retrieved_docs.content: The extraction of a single tooth is a direct dental procedure performed on the zuby (teeth), involving the removal of one tooth from the oral cavity.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 1 -- Community 225
retrieved_docs.content: Extraction of single tooth is a direct procedure performed on zuby (teeth), indicating that the teeth are the specific anatomical site for this dental intervention. Disorders of teeth and jaw are described as having zuby as their parent category, meaning that conditions affecting the teeth and jaw are organized under the broader classification of zuby. Consequently, zuby functions both as the procedural target for single‑tooth extraction and as the foundational element within the hierarchy of dental and jaw disorders, underscoring its central importance in dental treatment and pathology.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 1 -- Community 244
retrieved_docs.content: Extraction of single tooth is classified as a dental procedure, indicating that it has a relationship with the broader category of dental procedures. This connection underscores that the act of extracting a single tooth is a specific type of dental intervention within the overall field of dental procedures.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2341
retrieved_docs.content: The mentum presentation of the fetus, in which the fetal chin is the leading part during delivery, is classified as a مّجيء وَجهي (face presentation). Recognizing this presentation is important for obstetric assessment and delivery planning, as it influences the choice of birth method and the management of potential complications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 1 -- Community 226
retrieved_docs.content: Dental procedures are directly performed on zuby mléčné, indicating that primary (baby) teeth serve as the specific sites for these dental interventions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 2 -- Community 997
retrieved_docs.content: Intervillous hemorrhage of placenta is a disorder characterized by bleeding into the placental intervillous space and is regarded as a condition affecting the fetal membranes or amniotic fluid; it occurs in the context of a pregnancy in which a child is present.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1494
retrieved_docs.content: A gestational age of 29‑30 weeks completed is associated with a liveborn infant, indicating that pregnancies reaching this stage can result in a live birth.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 3 -- Community 1554
retrieved_docs.content: Allowed qualifier is identified as an abnormality of the amnion, indicating a deviation from normal amniotic development.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 0 -- Community 19
retrieved_docs.content: Extraction of single tooth is a dental procedure that directly targets teeth (zuby) and is recognized as part of the broader set of dental procedures. Dental procedures themselves are also performed directly on deciduous teeth (zuby mléčné). Disorders of teeth and jaw are categorized under the parent concept of teeth (zuby) and are specifically located in the deciduous dentition (zuby mléčné). Consequently, both the extraction of a single tooth and other dental procedures may be applied to permanent and baby teeth, while disorders of the teeth and jaw primarily affect the baby teeth.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2304
retrieved_docs.content: Early, first or unspecified trimester hemorrhage is classified as spotting complicating pregnancy, unspecified trimester.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 4 -- Community 2680
retrieved_docs.content: Short gestation, low birth weight, and fetal growth retardation are recognized as a related group of adverse perinatal outcomes and are classified together as a distinct clinical category.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 3 -- Community 1850
retrieved_docs.content: Abdominal pregnancy (حمل منتبذ) is clinically associated with laparotomy (شق البطن), because surgical entry into the abdominal cavity is frequently required for the diagnosis, evaluation, and definitive management of this rare ectopic gestation.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 1 -- Community 249
retrieved_docs.content: Dental procedures are performed directly on zuby mléčné (the primary or “milk” teeth), indicating that therapeutic dental interventions often target the primary dentition. Likewise, disorders of teeth and jaw are located in zuby mléčné, showing that many oral pathologies also affect these early‑life teeth. Together, these relationships emphasize that both clinical treatments and disease processes in pediatric dentistry are closely linked to the primary teeth, underscoring the importance of careful assessment and management of zuby mléčné in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 22 -- Level 3 -- Community 1940
retrieved_docs.content: Fetal monitoring is classified as تنظيم الجنين.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 14 -- Level 4 -- Community 2873
retrieved_docs.content: The condition referred to as اضطراب جنيني (fetal disorder) is classified as a type of مضاعفات الحمل (pregnancy complication). Recognizing that fetal disorders are encompassed within the broader category of pregnancy complications helps clinicians identify, monitor, and manage these issues during prenatal care, ensuring appropriate assessment and intervention for both maternal and fetal health.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 3 -- Community 2314
retrieved_docs.content: Malposition; malpresentation is classified as a protracted (prolapsed) arm of the fetus, with the episode of care unspecified or not applicable.
